examples.description: The defaults a new datasource starts with: HTTP Basic authentication with a 30s query timeout. Fill in root.url, root.basicAuthUser and secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword to get a working datasource.

basicAuth.description: Authenticate with a ServiceNow username and password. The instance URL and username live at the datasource root; only the password is secret (secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword).

legacyOAuthEnabled.description: Datasources created before authMethod existed store jsonData.oauthEnabled=true (deprecated) with no authMethod; GetAuthMethod resolves them to serviceNowOAuth. The credential shape is otherwise identical to the ServiceNow OAuth example.

serviceNowOAuth.description: ServiceNow OAuth is an OAuth2 resource-owner password grant, so it needs the account username/password AND the OAuth application's client id/secret. jsonData.oauthClientID is not secret; secureJsonData.oauthClientSecret is.
